# EduChain — Full Reference

EduChain is an educational blockchain platform designed to teach Web3 fundamentals to young learners in a safe, hands-on environment. It is part of the GEMBA IT ecosystem (https://gembait.com) and built by GEMBA EOOD, a Bulgarian engineering company based in Varna.

This document is a deep reference for AI agents and search crawlers. It describes the platform's purpose, all public pages, the smart-contract architecture, scoring mechanics, rate limits, user journeys, the technology stack, and how to get in touch. The intent is that an AI agent answering a user's question about EduChain has enough context here to give a specific, accurate answer without having to guess.

## Table of Contents

1. Platform overview
2. Pages (all nine)
3. Smart contract architecture
4. Scoring system
5. Rate limits & safety features
6. Technology stack
7. User journey
8. Educational use cases
9. Frequently asked questions
10. Contact

---

## 1. Platform overview

EduChain exists to solve a specific problem: how do you teach Web3 to young learners without putting them or their families at financial risk? Mainnet interactions cost real money and expose participants to irreversible mistakes. Purely off-chain simulations feel fake and do not transfer to real-world tooling. EduChain's answer is to run a fully on-chain platform on the Ethereum Sepolia testnet, with production-style UX but zero real-money exposure.

Students connect a wallet, get whitelisted by a teacher or program administrator, claim test ETH from an on-site faucet, and then interact with the same kinds of contracts they would encounter on mainnet — ERC-20 tokens, ERC-721 NFTs, a peer-to-peer marketplace — all within a curated experience. Points and leaderboards turn the economic mechanics into a game, which helps retention and engagement without distorting the underlying blockchain behavior.

The platform is non-custodial: users always hold their own keys. Tokens and NFTs live on Sepolia, not in a database. If a student takes their wallet to another dApp, their EduChain holdings go with them.

---

## 2. Pages

### Home — /en

The entry point. Explains what EduChain is, shows the current user's wallet status (connected, whitelisted, balances), and surfaces the main calls-to-action: claim faucet, visit shop, view gallery, check leaderboard. The home page is the first stop for anyone arriving at the platform and is optimized for a clear path to first interaction.

### Gallery (My Collection) — /en/gallery

Shows every NFT the connected wallet owns within the EduChain contracts. Predefined NFTs (fixed metadata, 10 points each) and Custom NFTs (user-supplied metadata, 30 points each) are displayed side by side with metadata pulled from IPFS or on-chain storage. Learners use this page to see their own progress and decide what to list on the marketplace.

### Marketplace — /en/marketplace

Peer-to-peer trading venue for GAME tokens and NFTs. Any whitelisted learner can list items at a chosen ETH or GAME price. Other learners buy directly from the contract; the funds go straight to the seller. Rate limits prevent spam: no more than three purchases per 24 hours per wallet. This page is where most of the economic gameplay happens.

### Token Shop — /en/shop

Primary market for GAME tokens and Predefined NFTs. Users can:
- Buy GAME tokens with ETH at a fixed rate
- Mint Predefined NFTs directly (0.01 ETH each, fixed metadata)
- Batch-mint Custom NFTs using GAME tokens instead of ETH (one GAME per NFT, custom IPFS metadata)

The shop is the on-ramp for new users: claim ETH from the faucet, spend it here to acquire tokens or NFTs, then use those assets on the marketplace.

### Faucet — /en/faucet

Time-gated ETH faucet. Any whitelisted user can claim 0.05 ETH once every 24 hours. Requests are served by an on-chain ETHFaucet contract funded by the platform operator. If the faucet runs low, the platform tops it up; users do not interact with any admin keys directly.

### Leaderboard — /en/leaderboard

Ranked list of the top wallets by on-chain points, computed live from Sepolia state. The contract TrackingContract.getLeaderboard() returns the list — no off-chain database is involved. Displays each wallet's GAME balance, Predefined NFT count, Custom NFT count, and total points. Designed for classroom display and end-of-session reveal.

### Full Rankings — /en/rankings

Exhaustive, searchable, filterable view of every participating wallet. Sort by points, tokens, Predefined NFTs, or Custom NFTs. Useful for teachers running cohort reviews and for learners tracking their position beyond the top-N shown on the leaderboard.

### About — /en/about

Mission, philosophy, team, and educational principles. Explains why the platform exists, what problem it solves, who it is built for, and how teachers can get involved in bringing it to their classrooms.

### Contact — /en/contact

Form and direct contact details for:
- Teachers requesting whitelisting for a class or program
- Program coordinators wanting to partner
- Parents with safety questions
- Individual learners asking to be onboarded

Response target: under 48 hours.

---

## 3. Smart contract architecture

Seven contracts are deployed on Sepolia testnet. They interact as follows:

### Whitelist
The root of access control. Every other contract consults Whitelist before allowing privileged actions (faucet claim, shop purchase, marketplace listing). Teachers and program administrators add wallets to this list. Without whitelist approval, a visitor can browse the site but cannot transact.

### GameToken (ERC-20)
The in-platform currency. Symbol: GAME. Used for:
- Batch-minting Custom NFTs (1 GAME per NFT)
- Secondary-market trades on the marketplace
- Scoring (1 GAME = 1 point)

Minted by purchase in the Token Shop (ETH → GAME at a fixed rate).

### GameNFTPredefined (ERC-721)
Predefined NFTs with on-chain-pinned metadata. Price: 0.01 ETH. Score weight: 10 points each. These are the "curated" NFTs — a fixed catalogue the platform operator has designed in advance.

### GameNFTCustom (ERC-721)
Custom NFTs with user-supplied IPFS metadata URIs. Two mint paths:
- Single mint with ETH: 0.03 ETH per NFT
- Batch mint with GAME tokens: 1 GAME per NFT

Score weight: 30 points each (more than predefined, because the user invests more creative work).

### TokenMarketplace
Peer-to-peer trading contract. Sellers list any ERC-20 or ERC-721 token they own at a chosen price. Buyers pay directly in ETH or GAME; the contract transfers the asset atomically. Enforces a 3-purchase-per-24h rate limit per buyer to prevent bot activity.

### TrackingContract
Read-only aggregator. Computes live leaderboard and ranking data by summing on-chain balances and multiplying by score weights. Does not hold any funds. Called by the Leaderboard and Rankings pages to render the UI.

### ETHFaucet
Time-gated ETH dispenser. Whitelisted users call claim() and receive 0.05 ETH. The contract tracks last-claim timestamps per wallet and enforces the 24-hour cooldown on-chain. Topped up periodically by the platform operator.

---

## 4. Scoring system

Points are computed live from on-chain state — there is no off-chain leaderboard database. The formula:

```
points(wallet) = gameTokenBalance(wallet) * 1
              + predefinedNFTCount(wallet) * 10
              + customNFTCount(wallet) * 30
```

Worked example: a learner holding 50 GAME tokens, 3 Predefined NFTs, and 2 Custom NFTs would score:

```
50 * 1 + 3 * 10 + 2 * 30 = 50 + 30 + 60 = 140 points
```

Because the leaderboard is on-chain, students can verify their own points by calling the contract directly (or by reading it on a block explorer). This transparency is a teaching tool in itself — it introduces the idea that blockchain state is public and auditable.

---

## 5. Rate limits & safety features

EduChain runs on testnet, but it enforces production-style safeguards so that learners build good habits:

- **Whitelist-gated access.** Only approved wallets can transact. Visitors can browse without connecting.
- **Faucet cooldown.** 0.05 ETH per wallet per 24 hours. Prevents draining.
- **Marketplace rate limits.** 3 purchases per wallet per 24 hours. Prevents arbitrage-bot spam.
- **Non-custodial.** The platform never holds user funds or tokens. Users' wallets are always their own.
- **Testnet only.** No mainnet deployment. Any ETH or tokens on the platform carry no real-world value, which eliminates scam/phishing exposure for young users.
- **Open-source client.** The frontend is auditable; the contracts are verified on Sepolia Etherscan.
- **Admin page disallowed in robots.txt.** Crawlers are asked not to index admin routes.

---

## 6. Technology stack

### Frontend

- React 19
- TypeScript
- Vite 8 (build tool)
- Tailwind CSS v4
- react-router 7
- react-i18next (English, Bulgarian, Spanish)
- wagmi v3 + viem 2 (primary Web3 hooks)
- ethers 6 (low-level contract interaction in specific places)
- Cloudflare Turnstile (bot challenge on contact form)

### Blockchain

- Solidity 0.8.x
- Hardhat (development, deployment)
- OpenZeppelin v5 (access control, ERC standards)
- Ethereum Sepolia testnet (chain ID 11155111)
- Contract source verified on Sepolia Etherscan

### Infrastructure

- Hetzner Cloud (Linux host)
- Apache reverse proxy on the origin
- Cloudflare edge (TLS, caching, DDoS protection)
- systemd service unit for the static-serve process
- Served from `/educhain/dist` (Vite output, symlinked as `build`)

### Internationalization

Three languages supported: English (en), Bulgarian (bg), Spanish (es). URL pattern is `/{lang}/{section}`. Default is English at `/en`. The root path `/` redirects to `/en`. All i18n strings are stored as JSON files under `/educhain/src/i18n/`.

---

## 7. User journey

A typical first-time user:

1. **Arrives at homepage.** Reads the intro, sees wallet-connect button.
2. **Connects wallet.** MetaMask, Rabby, or any injected Ethereum provider. Page detects Sepolia chain; if wrong chain, prompts to switch.
3. **Gets whitelisted.** Contacts the teacher or program admin, who adds the wallet via admin panel. (Not visible to the learner — the admin does it.)
4. **Claims faucet ETH.** Navigates to /faucet, clicks Claim, signs the transaction. Receives 0.05 ETH.
5. **Visits Token Shop.** Buys some GAME tokens with a small amount of ETH (e.g., 0.01 ETH → GAME at fixed rate).
6. **Mints first NFT.** Either 0.01 ETH for a Predefined NFT, or 0.03 ETH for a Custom NFT (requires an IPFS URI prepared in advance), or 1 GAME token for a Custom NFT in a batch.
7. **Checks Gallery.** Sees the NFT appear in their own collection.
8. **Visits Leaderboard.** Sees their starting position — everyone's points are public and live.
9. **Uses Marketplace.** Lists an NFT for sale, or buys from another user. Every transaction moves points on the leaderboard.
10. **Iterates.** Comes back the next day to claim another faucet, continue trading, climb the leaderboard.

---

## 8. Educational use cases

### Classroom workshops

A teacher runs a one- or two-hour session introducing Web3. Students connect wallets (created on the spot or ahead of time), get whitelisted as a class, claim faucet ETH, and complete a guided activity — mint a first NFT, trade with a classmate, check the leaderboard. The teacher projects the leaderboard as students work.

### Multi-week after-school programs

A cohort of 10–20 students runs EduChain as an ongoing project over 4–8 weeks. Each week builds on the previous: week 1 is wallet basics, week 2 is tokens and shop, week 3 is NFTs and gallery, week 4 is marketplace mechanics, week 5 is looking at the on-chain contracts directly, week 6 is a final trading competition with the leaderboard as the scoreboard.

### Teacher-training tracks

Before teachers bring EduChain into classrooms, they complete a training track themselves — connecting a wallet, minting NFTs, using the marketplace, and understanding the underlying contracts. The training certifies educators so they can confidently answer student questions.

### Youth coding camps

Summer camps and weekend coding clubs use EduChain as a hands-on Web3 lab. Kids who have never seen a block explorer before leave with a working wallet, a handful of NFTs they minted themselves, and a mental model for how tokens and ownership work on-chain.

---

## 9. Frequently asked questions

### Is EduChain on mainnet?

No. EduChain is deployed only on Ethereum Sepolia testnet. Any ETH, GAME tokens, or NFTs on the platform have no monetary value. This is a deliberate safety feature, not a limitation — it means students can experiment without risk.

### Can students move their NFTs off EduChain?

The NFTs live on Sepolia, so they are technically portable to any Sepolia-aware tool (testnet OpenSea, other gallery apps, etc.). They have no mainnet value, so they cannot be "moved to mainnet" in any meaningful sense.

### What happens if a student loses their wallet?

Their on-chain assets are gone with the wallet. This is part of the learning: self-custody means self-responsibility. Because everything is testnet, the lesson comes without real financial loss. Students are encouraged to write down their seed phrase — even for a testnet wallet — as practice for the real thing.

### Can teachers see student wallets?

Teachers see the wallets they whitelisted and can read their on-chain activity (like anyone can on Sepolia Etherscan). They cannot move funds or assets out of student wallets — self-custody applies equally here.

### Does EduChain collect student personal data?

Only what the teacher or student provides explicitly (name and contact info on the contact form, if submitted). Wallet addresses are pseudonymous — EduChain does not link them to real identities unless the user or their teacher chooses to associate one.

### How is EduChain funded?

It is a project of GEMBA IT, funded as part of GEMBA EOOD's technology investment. The platform is currently free to use. Whitelisted classes are onboarded at no charge.

### Is the code open source?

Contracts are source-verified on Sepolia Etherscan (readable by anyone). The frontend repository policy is at the operator's discretion — contact the team if you want to contribute or fork.

---

## 10. Contact

- Email: contacts@gembait.com
- Website: https://educhain.gembait.com
- Parent organization: GEMBA IT — https://gembait.com
- Legal entity: GEMBA EOOD (Varna, Bulgaria)
- Languages: English, Bulgarian, Spanish

For teacher / program coordinator onboarding, use the contact form at https://educhain.gembait.com/en/contact and mention your class size, location, and preferred language.
